findings.description: The NC Marine Fisheries logo/seal in the HTML appears smaller and slightly different in rendering compared to the PDF version.
findings.evidence: PDF shows a larger, more detailed state seal with '200 years' celebration badge. HTML shows a similar but smaller version.

findings.description: Page 2 of the HTML has excessive whitespace/blank space in the middle of the page, splitting content that flows continuously in the PDF. The HTML appears to repeat the header/letterhead mid-page unnecessarily.
findings.evidence: PDF page 2 flows continuously from page 1 content. HTML page 2 shows a large blank area followed by a repeated header block (ROY COOPER, Governor, etc.) and then re-renders the proclamation content.

findings.description: The HTML page 2 appears to duplicate the proclamation header and beginning content, showing what seems like a restart of the document mid-page.
findings.evidence: PDF page 2 is a continuation from page 1 with gear restrictions and general information. HTML page 2 shows the footer, then a second instance of the full header with ROY COOPER/ELIZABETH S. BISER/KATHY B. RAWLS, the FF-52-2023 number, and the PROCLAMATION title again, followed by the RE: line and introductory paragraph repeated.

findings.description: The right-aligned header block (ROY COOPER Governor, ELIZABETH S. BISER Secretary, KATHY B. RAWLS Director) positioning differs slightly between PDF and HTML.
findings.evidence: PDF has these right-aligned at the top-right corner. HTML has them centered-right but the general positioning is similar.

findings.description: The section numbering format differs slightly - Roman numerals in PDF use period-space formatting while HTML uses period-space but with slightly different indentation.
findings.evidence: PDF shows 'I.  SIZE LIMIT' with underlined formatting, HTML shows similar but with slightly different spacing.

findings.description: HTML page 4 contains a large blank space in the middle, splitting what is continuous content in the PDF across an unnecessary gap with a repeated footer/header.
findings.evidence: PDF page 3 flows continuously with general information items. HTML page 4 has a footer block mid-content followed by large whitespace before resuming content.

findings.description: The signature on the HTML version appears to be missing (only text 'By:' with a line, but no actual signature image).
findings.evidence: PDF page 5 shows a handwritten signature of Kathy B. Rawls. HTML page 6 shows 'By:' with a blank line and name/title text but no signature image.

findings.description: The statute reference '143B-289.52' appears in the PDF general information section IV.A but in the HTML the formatting of the citation list slightly differs in line breaks.
findings.evidence: PDF shows the citations on two lines with specific breaks. HTML shows them in a slightly different flow.

findings.description: The document only uses h1 for the title 'PROCLAMATION'. All subsequent section headings (I. SIZE LIMIT, II. HARVEST PERIOD AND LIMITS, III. GEAR RESTRICTIONS, IV. GENERAL INFORMATION) are rendered as bold/underlined list items rather than using h2/h3 heading tags, resulting in a flat heading hierarchy.
findings.evidence:
PDF has clear hierarchical sections I through IV with sub-items. HTML uses
    with
  1. ... instead of proper heading elements like h2 for section titles.

findings.description: The alt text for the page 2 header image is empty, while the page 3 image alt text describes a 'Composite layered image including signature of Kathy B. Rawls' which may be inaccurate since page 3 of the PDF shows the same NC Marine Fisheries logo/header, with the signature appearing separately below.
findings.evidence: HTML: alt="" for page 2 image; alt="Composite layered image including signature of Kathy B. Rawls" for page 3 image. The page 3 PDF shows the standard footer and then the signature block separately.

findings.description: The page footer (State of North Carolina | Division of Marine Fisheries | address | phone) appears 3 times in the HTML, and the page header image also appears 3 times. While this reflects the 3-page PDF, consolidation would be more appropriate for HTML output.
findings.evidence: Footer block with '3441 Arendell Street | P.O. Box 769 | Morehead City, North Carolina 28557 | 252-726-7021' appears 3 times in the HTML.

findings.description: The URL for the eCFR link is split across two anchor tags due to page break handling, but both point to the correct URL. The link text in the first anchor shows a truncated URL ending with 'part-' while the second shows '648/subpart-i'. This is a minor formatting issue.
findings.evidence: HTML: first <a> has text 'https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-50/part-', second <a> has text '648/subpart-i'. PDF shows the full URL 'https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-50/part-648/subpart-i'.
